SCHEDULE OF EVENTS - ANNUAL MEETING 2026

blog

American Academy of Periodontology

112th Annual Meeting
Oct. 29 – Nov. 1, 2026
Seattle, Washington

REGISTER NOW

The 112th Annual Meeting is being held in collaboration with the Japanese Society of Periodontology and Japanese Academy of Clinical Periodontology.

Program schedule and content is subject to change. Stay tuned to for the latest information.

*All times below are listed in Pacific Standard Time.

+ Wednesday, Oct 28
08:00 AM - 05:00 PM
EV4: Educator Symposium (Ticketed Course)
+ Thursday, Oct 29
08:00 AM - 11:00 AM
ABP1: American Board Review Part 1
Clinical Innovations and Periodontal Research Symposium sponsored by District 8
08:00 AM - 12:00 PM
EV1: Sedation and Anxiety Management Part 1 (Ticketed Course)
EV24: Elevating Scholarship: Peer Reviewing and Writing for the AAP Journals (Ticketed Course)
Pan Pacific Session (Non-CE)
01:00 PM - 04:00 PM
ABP2: American Board Review Part 2
01:00 PM - 04:30 PM
Balint Orban Memorial Competition
01:00 PM - 05:00 PM
Corporate Forum
EV2: Sedation and Anxiety Management Part 2 (Ticketed Course)
+ Friday, Oct 30
08:00 AM - 10:00 AM
GS1: Perio Pioneers: Leading the Future
10:45 AM - 11:30 AM
IP1: Advances in Periodontics
NCE1: Digital Approach to Manage Excessive Gingival Display
NCE2: Clinical Insights into the Periodontal-Respiratory Interface: Breathless Connections Reconsidered
01:30 PM - 03:00 PM
FCE1: Lost Art or Lost Cause: Treating Furcations
FCE2: When Implants Go Rogue – What’s Your Plan?
FCE3: Robert J. Genco Perio-Systemic Symposium: Fighting the Fire of Inflammation: Novel Strategies for Host Modulation in Clinical Therapy
IP2: Advances in Periodontics
03:30 PM - 05:30 PM
GS2: Guided Bone Regeneration: from Principles to Practice
+ Saturday, Oct 31
08:00 AM - 10:00 AM
GS3: Going for Gold: Advancements in Soft Tissue Grafting for Root Coverage
08:00 AM - 05:00 PM
EV3: Dental Hygiene Symposium (Ticketed Course)
10:45 AM - 11:30 AM
IP3: Rising Stars
NCE4: Navigating Navigation: Why you should be Dynamic
NCE5: Decoding the Bone Threshold for Sinus Augmentation Success
01:30 PM - 03:00 PM
FCE4: Elevate Your Team for Practice Excellence
FCE5: Where Oral Pathology Meets Periodontics: Identify, Diagnose, and Manage
FCE6: The Edentulous Dilemma: Options for Full Arch Rehabilitation
03:30 PM - 05:30 PM
GS4: Seamless Synergy: Crafting the Ultimate Interdisciplinary Dream Team
+ Sunday, Nov 1
08:00 AM - 08:30 AM
CCE1: Innovative Periodontal-Orthodontic Strategies: Exploring Interdisciplinary Dentofacial Therapy with Implants, Prosthodontics and SFOT
08:45 AM - 09:15 AM
CCE2: Minimally Invasive Regenerative Therapy for Severe Periodontitis in Elderly Patients
09:30 AM - 10:00 AM
CCE3: Soft Tissue Management of the Malpositioned Implant
10:45 AM - 12:45 PM
GS5: BEC Results Primary Prevention of Peri-implant Diseases

*Please note that all information in this program including agenda, timing, faculty, and/or program content is subject to change.

ADA CERP/CE Credit

The American Academy of Periodontology is an ADA CERP Recognized Provider. ADA CERP is a service of the American Dental Association to assist dental professionals in identifying quality providers of continuing dental education. ADA CERP does not approve or endorse individual courses or instructors, nor does it imply acceptance of credit hours by boards of dentistry. Concerns or complaints about a CE provider may be directed to the provider or to the Commission for Continuing Education Provider Recognition at ADA.org/CERP.

The American Academy of Periodontology designates this activity for 28.00 continuing education credits. For all CE activities, 0.25 credit hours will be awarded for each 15 minutes of activity time, not including breaks, etc. The AAP cautions participants in CE courses about the potential risks of using limited knowledge when incorporating new techniques and procedures in their practice, especially when the course has not provided supervised clinical experience.
